messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following logistics and freight company privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"FreightCo collects shipper and recipient names, addresses, and contact details to arrange and track shipments. We also collect driver names and GPS location data for fleet management. Customer data is processed under our contract with you. Driver data is processed under the employment contract and legitimate interest for fleet safety. Shipment records are retained for 7 years for tax and legal purposes. Driver location data is retained for 30 days. You have the right to access, correct, and erase your data. Contact dataprotection@freightco.com. Our DPO is at dpo@freightco.com. We use SAP TM (EU-hosted) under a DPA. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) for customer data. Employment contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) and legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) for driver data. Multiple bases correctly applied to distinct data subject categories.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Driver GPS data collected continuously could reveal religious observance, health-related patterns, or union activity. No acknowledgement or safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Shipment arrangement, tracking, and fleet management/safety are clearly stated as distinct purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate to freight logistics and fleet management purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Shipment records: 7 years (tax/legal). Driver location: 30 days. Both clearly stated and justified.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), and erasure (Art. 17) listed. Rights to portability (Art. 20), restriction (Art. 18), and objection (Art. 21) absent. Objection is particularly relevant for driver legitimate interest processing.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] SAP TM named as logistics management processor with EU hosting and DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU-hosted SAP TM confirms EEA processing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@freightco.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (driver location data inference risk not addressed), Art. 15-21 (portability, restriction, objection rights absent)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that driver GPS patterns may reveal sensitive information and state appropriate safeguards
- Add rights to portability, restriction, and objection — and ensure the right to object is prominently stated for driver legitimate interest processing
